approach: Model the chair using cube primitives for main components, cylinder for rounded edges if needed, and hull() to create tapered legs. Use translate and rotate to position components. Employ union for assembly and potentially difference for joinery details.

steps.description: Create the seat as a rectangular cube
Create four tapered legs using hull() between two cubes of different sizes
Create vertical back posts (two tapered columns)
Create horizontal back slats (3-4 rectangular bars)
Create horizontal support rails between legs (stretchers)
Add optional rounded edges using minkowski with small sphere

notes: The chair has tapered legs which are best created using hull() between differently-sized cubes at top and bottom positions. The ladder-back design requires iterating to create multiple horizontal slats. Support rails can be created as rotated cubes positioned between the legs. Consider using modules for reusable components like legs. Set $fn for smoother cylinders/spheres if used for rounding.
